reason_thou:
1. Understanding the Issue:
The crash occurs in BoUpSLP::removeInstructionsAndOperands during horizontal reduction vectorization. The assertion checks that each instruction being erased either has no users or all its users are already marked deleted. In the reproducer, scalar compare instructions (%cmp80... and %tobool...) are scheduled for deletion while they are still used by a chain of select instructions implementing a boolean reduction. Those select instructions are part of the reduction but are kept temporarily (they are placed in UserIgnoreList). The validation code earlier (vectorizeTree) explicitly allows erasing scalars whose users are in UserIgnoreList, but removeInstructionsAndOperands' assertion does not account for this case. As a result, the assertion fires even though the situation is intended and handled elsewhere.

2. Analyzing opt's Log:
The debug log shows lines such as:
SLP: \tvalidating user:  %cmp80.1.2.i.i = select ...
SLP: \tErasing scalar:  %cmp80.1.2.i.i193 = icmp sgt i32 0, 0.
Similar patterns for other compares and boolean icmps. All scalar compares are pushed into RemovedInsts. Their users (the select chain) remain alive and are not marked deleted. Because this is a horizontal reduction, UserIgnoreList is active (set earlier when matching reduction). No replacement of those select operands has yet occurred before removeInstructionsAndOperands runs.

3. Root Cause Analysis:
Code in vectorizeTree permits erasing scalars whose users are either vectorized tree nodes, ignored reduction nodes (UserIgnoreList), or already deleted. The assertion in removeInstructionsAndOperands only permits users that are deleted (isDeleted). It omits the UserIgnoreList case. Thus, in horizontal reduction mode, legitimate live users in UserIgnoreList trigger a false assertion failure. The semantic expectation is that deletion of underlying scalar instructions can proceed while reduction root/select nodes are processed later; hence the assertion must allow users from UserIgnoreList similarly to the earlier validation.

4. Proposed Edit Point(s):
Edit lines 2748-2753 in SLPVectorizer.cpp to extend the assertion predicate to accept users contained in UserIgnoreList. This mirrors the earlier NDEBUG check (lines ~16096-16102) and keeps consistency. Modified assertion:
assert((I->use_empty() || all_of(I->uses(), [&](Use &U) {
  if (auto *UsrI = dyn_cast<Instruction>(U.getUser()))
    return isDeleted(UsrI) || (UserIgnoreList && UserIgnoreList->contains(UsrI));
  return true; })) && "trying to erase instruction with users.");
This change preserves correctness while preventing the spurious abort.

Rationale: We are not weakening real safety because the pass logic intentionally tolerates remaining users in UserIgnoreList (reduction tree nodes) and will handle them later. The assertion was overly strict compared to earlier validation logic and inconsistent with horizontal reduction flow.

5. Conclusion:
By adjusting the assertion to recognize permitted remaining users (those in UserIgnoreList), we align removeInstructionsAndOperands with the established invariant used elsewhere in vectorizeTree. This prevents the abort for valid reduction patterns like the reproducer while keeping the safety property that we do not erase instructions still used by arbitrary live IR outside the managed reduction tree. No other logic changes required.
